install_patterns: pip install -r requirements.txt (backend README and run commands)
curl -X POST against local dev endpoint (not curl|bash; no remote pipe-to-shell observed)
risk_notes: All four npm dependencies (@vitejs/plugin-react, vite, react, react-dom) use 'latest' instead of pinned versions — builds are non-reproducible and vulnerable to upstream changes or supply-chain hijack.
No lockfile (package-lock.json/yarn.lock) is shown for the frontend, compounding the 'latest' risk.
Python requirements are pinned to exact versions but lack hashes (no pip --require-hashes / no requirements.lock), so a compromised PyPI index could still substitute artifacts.
frontend/package.json has @vitejs/plugin-react and vite listed under 'dependencies' rather than 'devDependencies', enlarging the production dependency surface.

WebSocket URL (BROKERAGE_WS_URL) and trading parameters are configured via .env; .env.example uses a placeholder wss://api.brokerage.com/live but README/production notes mention real broker auth will be added — risk of secrets being committed to .env if not gitignored.
CORS_ORIGINS is read from env with a fallback wildcard-like permissive default (["http://localhost:5173"]); misconfiguration could broaden allowed origins.
SQLite database file path (sqlite:///./trades.db) is relative — could end up in unintended locations depending on working directory.
uvicorn is bound to 0.0.0.0 in README run commands, exposing the dev server on all interfaces.
